Title: Supplier continuous-monitoring gap
Description: Supplier risk program centered on annual reassessments only; no continuous monitoring of Tier-1 or Tier-2 vendors beyond AWS/Okta/Snowflake (which feed Vanta automatically). Reassessment cadence at 60% Q1 2026 against ≥90% target. The Change Healthcare ALPHV/BlackCat event (Feb-Mar 2024) is the primary illustrative case.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — redesign program with continuous-monitoring expectations; expand Vanta feeds to Tier-2 vendors
Notes: FY26 priority 7 in backgrounder; Audit & Risk Committee approved redesign Dec 2025

Title: Recovery testing depth
Description: DR test program is annual and scope-limited to one critical service. Recovery tabletops historically conflated with IR tabletops. Service-tier RTO/RPO commitments (P1 4h/15m) not yet exercised against full-region failure scenario.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — FY26 plan adds 2 recovery tabletops + 1 additional full failover; FY27 plan to introduce game-day-style exercises
Notes: Board-acknowledged weakness; KPI tracking shows 1 full failover/year persisting through Q1 2026

Title: Detection alert tuning debt
Description: Tier-1 SIEM alerts have approximately 28% false-positive rate. Detection use cases for legacy ETL cluster are incomplete. Alert response runbooks for Snowflake compute anomalies still draft.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — Q1 2026 dedicated tuning sprint; expand DE.AE coverage with new use cases
Notes: Tuning sprint approved; one engineer half-allocated for Q1 2026

Title: PHI flow visibility into legacy ETL cluster
Description: Legacy on-premises ETL cluster at Raleigh HQ supports a deprecating EHR integration for two long-tenured customers. Cluster is not in central asset inventory automation; data flow into Aurora is not fully instrumented; manual patching only.
Treatment Strategy: Avoid — decommission cluster Q4 2026; compensating controls in place per CRA-LegacyETL-2025
Notes: Customer migration to modern integration in flight; 60% complete as of Q4 2025

Title: Third-party access reviews
Description: SaaS service-account access (non-human identities) is reviewed inconsistently; no automated discovery for OAuth-style access grants. Quarterly human access reviews are reliable (Q1 2026 KPI 98%) but service-account scope is gap.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — Vanta access governance pilot Q2 2026; SCIM cleanup project in design
Notes: Discovered during Q3 2025 SOC 2 fieldwork; auditor noted no exception but flagged in management response

Title: Warm-DR readiness for Population Insights
Description: Snowflake-based Population Insights module has weaker DR posture than core Aurora-backed services. Data replication is asynchronous with 2-4h lag and warm-standby compute is not pre-provisioned in us-west-2.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — FY27 plan to introduce hot-standby Snowflake account in us-west-2; documented in P2 service tier; current RTO 24h is achievable under existing posture
Notes: Population Insights is P2 (not P1) so current posture is within commitment; risk acceptance considered but rejected in favor of treatment in FY27

Title: Insider-threat program maturity
Description: No formal insider-threat program. Background-check refresh on a 5-year cycle for engineers and security staff is in design. UEBA capabilities exist in Datadog but rules are not tuned for insider-threat use cases.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — UEBA tuning Q3 2026; insider-threat policy POL-IT-01 in draft; baseline behavioral analytics on production access logs
Notes: GC and CPO partnership required; legal review of monitoring scope ongoing

Title: Patient-portal CIAM hardening (Auth0)
Description: Patient-facing portal CIAM is on Auth0 (separate from workforce Okta). Some legacy customers still permit username+password without MFA on patient-facing logins because customer healthcare populations include low-tech users. Risk-based step-up auth in design.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — risk-based step-up authentication rollout Q3 2026; customer-tier MFA requirement contractual change for new contracts
Notes: Patient breach impact lower than provider account compromise but still in scope

Title: SBOM completeness for OSS dependencies
Description: Software bill of materials (SBOM) generation for production services was incomplete; some Go services and the legacy ETL cluster were not covered. Closed Q3 2025 with full coverage via Snyk + GitHub Advanced Security.
Notes: Closed Q3 2025; verification by Coalfire HITRUST assessor confirmed

Title: Workforce phishing susceptibility
Description: Quarterly phishing simulation click rate at 7.2% baseline (Q1 2024) against ≤3% target. Pebble Phish credential phishing succeeded in March 2024.
Treatment Strategy: Mitigate — quarterly simulations + targeted retraining + FIDO2 mandate
Notes: Click rate at 2.4% Q4 2025; report rate 48%; closed as a register entry but program continues

Inherent severity rating: Low / Medium / High / Critical
